data.title: UK Financial Authority Nears Completion of Crypto Regulation Consultations
data.description:
The United Kingdom's Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) is approaching the final stages of its consultation process for significant proposed cryptocurrency regulations, as it continues to develop the government's crypto roadmap. According to Cointelegraph, the FCA announced on Friday that it is now seeking feedback on ten regulatory proposals, marking the "final step" in its consultations on potential rules for the sector.

The FCA emphasized that these proposals aim to foster an open, sustainable, and competitive crypto market that people can trust. However, the agency acknowledged that risks remain and expressed a desire for a market where innovation can thrive, while ensuring that individuals understand the associated risks. The FCA clarified that regulation should not attempt to eliminate all risks, but rather help investors comprehend them.

The proposed regulations encompass various aspects of the crypto market, including business standards of conduct, credit-based crypto purchases, regulatory reporting, asset safeguarding, and retail collateral treatment for borrowing crypto, among other areas. The deadline for feedback on these proposals is set for March 12. Initially outlined in December, the FCA's package of proposals reflects its intent to regulate the crypto market in a manner similar to traditional finance.

The FCA has reported "significant progress" in refining the regulatory details as part of the government's roadmap. Earlier this month, the agency announced a timeline for crypto asset service providers to register under its new licensing regime. The FCA expects the application period to commence in September 2026, with the timeline to be confirmed in due course. This licensing regime will impose stricter oversight and constraints on crypto firms, requiring them to obtain FCA authorization to operate within the UK.

data.title: Da Hongfei Responds to Zhang Zhengwen's Statements on Neo Foundation
data.description:
Da Hongfei has publicly responded to recent statements made by Zhang Zhengwen, describing them as a "serious distortion of facts" and accusing Zhang of presenting "only part of the truth." According to Odaily, Da Hongfei is still seeking "limited cooperation" with Zhang.

Da Hongfei clarified that the "mechanism" Zhang proposed was essentially a centralized management model similar to the Neo Foundation's treasury, requiring his approval for every transaction and expenditure. In contrast, Da Hongfei suggested that starting this year, the Neo Foundation and NGD would disclose their financial reports quarterly to enhance transparency and detail.

He further explained that he had informed Zhang and other community members about the possibility of publishing news and blog content on neo.org and had offered the use of the @Neo_Blockchain account to expand their influence. However, this background information was not fully disclosed, leading to a presentation of "partial facts." Da Hongfei emphasized the need to document all communication thoroughly in the future.

Regarding financial arrangements, Da Hongfei revealed that with Zhang's consent, NGD has conducted a transaction with the Neo Foundation treasury, initially amounting to $1 million, executed at spot prices. The aim is to increase NGD's equity share to align stakeholder interests and provide Zhang with operational funds for promoting the Neo mainnet.

Da Hongfei reiterated his stance on "limited cooperation," advocating for collaboration where goals align and independent progress where differences exist. He believes this is the only viable path to resolving Neo's governance stalemate and revitalizing its ecosystem. He urged Zhang not to misinterpret his statements further.

data.title: Aperture Finance Investigates Contract Vulnerability Exploitation
data.description:
Aperture Finance has reported a vulnerability exploitation affecting its V3/V4 contracts, according to PANews. The company announced on the X platform that it has halted core functions in its front-end application to prevent further authorizations and is collaborating with security partners to investigate the root cause of the incident. Users are advised to revoke all authorizations on the Ethereum mainnet for the contract address 0xD83d960deBEC397fB149b51F8F37DD3B5CFA8913 to ensure wallet security.

Previously, BlockSec detected an attack on Aperture Finance resulting in a loss of approximately $3.67 million.

data.title: Gold Outperforms Ethereum Over Five Years, Reaches $5,000 Milestone
data.description: AB Kuai.Dong posted on X. Gold has surpassed Ethereum in returns over the past five years, reaching a significant milestone of $5,000. Tether, the parent company of the stablecoin USDT, has reportedly benefited from using cryptocurrency income to accumulate gold reserves. This strategic move has proven advantageous as gold continues to outperform major cryptocurrencies in terms of long-term returns.

data.title: ZachXBT questioned Circle's inadequate response to security incidents, claiming that the USDC ecosystem poses a risk of centralization.
data.description: Chain detective ZachXBT published an article on the X platform questioning Circle's handling of the security incident, stating that "history has proven Circle to be a bad actor." ZachXBT stated that approximately 10 hours ago, the SwapNet contract on the Base network was attacked, resulting in a loss of approximately $13 million USDC. About $3 million of this USDC remains in an address that Circle could freeze (0x6cAad74121bF602e71386505A4687f310e0D833e), but no effective action has been taken to address this issue. He further questioned Circle's failure to take timely action to protect user interests after the attack on user assets, and asked why developers are still willing to build applications based on USDC, arguing that its centralized risks and governance methods have impacted trust within the ecosystem.

data.title: South Korea’s Coinone selling major stakeholder shares, in talks with overseas crypto exchanges: report
data.description:
The crypto exchange is in talks with overseas crypto exchanges and local financial institutions for the sale, local media reported.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/386967/south-koreas-coinone-selling-shares?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Coinone, South Korea's third-largest cryptocurrency exchange, is planning to sell its major shareholder's stake and is in talks with overseas cryptocurrency exchanges regarding potential investments.
data.description: Coinone, South Korea's third-largest cryptocurrency exchange, is exploring the sale of a portion of its shares, including those held by its chairman and largest shareholder, Cha Myung-hoon. Reports indicate that Coinone is considering various options to sell Cha's total 53.4% ​​stake, including his personal 19.14% and a 3.43% held by his affiliated company, The One Group. Coinone stated that it has communicated with several major overseas cryptocurrency exchanges and local financial institutions regarding equity investments, but "no concrete decisions have been made yet." The report notes that Cha recently returned to the company's management team after resigning as CEO and taking a four-month hiatus, and his return may be related to preparations for the share sale and a larger-scale transaction. Furthermore, the shares held by the second-largest shareholder, local gaming company Com2Us (which accumulated a 38.42% stake between 2021 and 2022), may also be included in the potential transaction. Financially, Coinone has been consistently losing money in recent quarters, with a book value of approximately 75.2 billion won (about US$52.2 million) at the end of the third quarter, lower than its previous valuation of approximately 94.4 billion won. According to industry insiders, Coinbase may be interested in participating in this equity transaction, and its executive team is expected to visit South Korea this week to hold talks with Coinone and other local companies. However, Coinone has not yet officially confirmed any completed transactions. (TheBlock)

data.title: Probability of U.S.-Japan Currency Intervention Rises, Expert Says
data.description: On January 26, Masahiko Loo, a senior fixed income strategist at State Street Global Advisors, indicated that the likelihood of a joint U.S.-Japan intervention in currency rates has significantly increased. According to BlockBeats, Loo highlighted the importance of Friday's interest rate check report, noting that historically, such checks by the Ministry of Finance have been precursors to action. He warned that if no subsequent measures are taken, speculative pressure could intensify as the market tests the authorities' resolve by further weakening the yen. Regardless of timing, Loo emphasized that the market's 'bottom line' is clear: 162, the level at which the last intervention occurred.

data.title: Nansen Completes Integration with Sui Ecosystem
data.description:
Nansen, a blockchain data analysis platform, has announced the completion of its integration with the Sui ecosystem, enhancing real-time on-chain visibility for developers, institutions, and researchers. According to Odaily, this integration includes a dedicated Sui ecosystem dashboard, allowing users to track asset flows, application usage, and participant behavior, facilitating a clearer understanding of financial and collaborative dynamics within the Sui ecosystem.

Future updates will introduce features like Token God Mode and Nansen Profiler, aimed at analyzing token performance, holder structures, and the behavior of smart money and institutional wallets. Nansen states that its AI-driven data analysis capabilities enable Sui ecosystem participants to access actionable on-chain signals within a unified view, allowing them to assess project activity, ecosystem trends, and make informed decisions based on verifiable data.

data.title: The Telegram mini-game Capybobo 2 has started its beta test. Players can earn trendy toys and token rewards by consuming PYBOBO.
data.description: Capybobo 2 has announced the start of its second-generation closed beta test, which will run until January 28th. Players can experience the game mechanics in advance during the test period. In Capybobo 2, PYBOBO is the only way to acquire trendy toys. Players consume PYBOBO to capture trendy toys, raise them to level up, and then unlock mining power to produce CROCO tokens. The game uses a competitive deflationary mechanism, with a fixed and decreasing daily output of $CROCO, which is distributed among players through competition for computing power. According to the WHS computing power model, the higher the level of the trendy toy, the more exponentially the mining efficiency increases. All PYBOBO consumption and CROCO output data are recorded on the blockchain in real time using VWA technology, making them transparent and verifiable. This test adopts a double return mechanism. Players participating in the test period will receive double the resources for raising their toys in the official server, resulting in higher output in the mining competition. In addition, on January 26, Bybit announced the launch of spot trading for $PYBOBO; PYBOBO had previously been listed on KuCoin on January 23.

data.title: Trader Utilizes High Leverage for Bitcoin Short Position
data.description:
On January 26, a trader known as 'God of War' executed a short position on 57 BTC using 40x leverage. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the average entry price for this position was $87,716.9, and it is currently showing a slight unrealized profit.

The trader's address has completed 207 transactions, with only five resulting in a total loss of $5,195.36. The remaining 202 transactions were closed with profits, leading to a total account profit of $447,800.

data.title: Institutional Investors See Bitcoin as Undervalued, Survey Finds
data.description: A recent report indicates that a significant majority of institutional investors perceive Bitcoin as undervalued. According to NS3.AI, 71% of institutional investors hold this belief, compared to 60% of retail investors. The survey also highlights that 80% of institutional investors are inclined to hold or purchase more Bitcoin if the market experiences a further 10% decline. Furthermore, more than half of all investors consider the current crypto market to be in an accumulation phase or bear market. Potential rate cuts by the Federal Reserve are anticipated to benefit risk assets.

data.title: An address received $10.32 million worth of HYPE from Galaxy Digital OTC, while its ENA holdings suffered a paper loss of $15.44 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, two hours ago, an address (0xd4d) received 465,000 HYPE tokens from Galaxy Digital OTC, worth $10.32 million. Additionally, this address received 56.12 million ENA tokens from Ethena Lab Wallet 2.5 months ago, at the time worth $24.76 million; the value has now decreased to $9.32 million, resulting in a paper loss of $15.44 million.

data.title: Nansen now supports Sui, providing real-time ecosystem data and on-chain insights.
data.description: On-chain data analytics platform Nansen announced the completion of its integration with Sui, officially adding support to the Sui ecosystem and providing developers, institutions, and researchers with more comprehensive real-time on-chain visibility. According to the announcement, the integration includes a dedicated Sui ecosystem dashboard, allowing users to track asset flows, application usage, and participant behavior, helping to more intuitively understand the funds and collaborative relationships within the Sui ecosystem. Subsequent phases will include features such as Token God Mode and Nansen Profiler for analyzing token performance, holder structure, and smart money and institutional wallet behavior. Nansen stated that through AI-driven data analytics capabilities, Sui ecosystem participants can obtain actionable on-chain signals in a unified view, enabling them to assess project activity, ecosystem trends, and make decisions based on verifiable data.

data.title: Infinex will conduct a Patron INX snapshot on January 30th, with each Patron receiving 100,000 INX tokens.
data.description: Cross-chain DeFi aggregation platform Infinex tweeted that it will take a Patron INX snapshot at 15:00 on January 30th. According to the rules, users will receive 100,000 INX for every 1 Patron held. Infinex stated that after the TGE (Transactions for the End of the Year) event, all rights associated with Patron, including fee discounts, priority access, and community access, will be migrated to the INX token, while Patron NFTs will be used for commemorative purposes.

data.title: Bitcoin Struggles to Maintain Key Moving Average, Signaling Market Correction
data.description: Matrixport's recent analysis highlights Bitcoin's inability to maintain levels above the crucial 21-week moving average, indicating the market is still in a correction phase. According to NS3.AI, despite a tactical rebound towards the end of December, Bitcoin has not succeeded in reclaiming and holding prices above this moving average. The firm observes that there are currently no definitive indicators suggesting a sustained new uptrend in the near future.

data.title: Winter Storm Fernan Disrupts Bitcoin Mining Operations in the US
data.description: The US winter storm 'Fernan' has significantly affected Foundry USA's Bitcoin mining pool, leading to a substantial decrease in its hashrate. According to NS3.AI, the hashrate has dropped by approximately 60% since Friday, resulting in around 200 exahashes per second (EH/s) going offline. This disruption has caused a slowdown in Bitcoin block production, with the average block time increasing to 12 minutes.
